fresh_facts.claim: The IRGC claimed on Thursday, April 3, 2026, to have struck a US F-35 stealth fighter jet over central Iran using air defenses and released images of wreckage.
Iran is deploying large numbers of low-cost drones in swarms to target critical infrastructure like desalination plants in Gulf states as a strategy of horizontal escalation.
US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth threatened to strike Iran extremely hard with B-2s, B-52s, B-1s, predator drones, and fighters.
The US Army Chief of Staff was ousted mid-war during the fifth week of conflict with Iran.
President Pezeshkian faces severe political deadlock after signaling conditional willingness to end the war with serious disagreements emerging with IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi.
Mojtaba Khamenei, the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son, was appointed as his successor by Tehran's Assembly of Experts following the Supreme Leader's death on February 28, 2026.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council under pressure from IRGC commanders, replacing the assassinated Ali Larijani.
Trump stated the U.S. and Iran held productive talks, with Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio joining diplomatic envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied negotiations with the US occurred, while the foreign ministry acknowledged friendly states conveyed US requests for talks.
A fifteen-point U.S. peace proposal was reportedly delivered via Pakistan, encompassing Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear program rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran was reviewing a US peace proposal but insisted it did not intend to negotiate.
Iran stated a condition for war termination includes fighting cessation on all fronts and guarantees against future attacks.
Trump announced a five-day pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure, with more than two thousand additional U.S. Marines expected in the Middle East by the pause's end.
Iran's economy exhibits structural symptoms of currency collapse, with the country losing faith in its own currency and experiencing a shift in its monetary regime.
Iranian officials increasingly portray control of the Strait of Hormuz as a financial asset to offset war costs.
Recent protests represent the most serious domestic unrest the Islamic Republic has ever faced.
Iranian state television has escalated messaging by warning citizens against revealing official locations and labeling protesters as enemy combatants.
An Iranian armed forces spokesperson warned that adversaries would not be safe in tourist centers worldwide.
The Revolutionary Guard has effectively assumed control over key state functions within Iran's government.
Mojtaba Khamenei has only recently issued statements after days of absence, with media efforts to reinforce his image as a wartime leader.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran escalates military operations with drone swarms and claimed F-35 strike while simultaneously engaged in diplomatic signaling through back-channels, creating a strategic ambiguity amid deepening internal fractures between civilian leadership and IRGC, succession instability under Mojtaba Khamenei, currency collapse, and most severe domestic unrest in Islamic Republic history.
regime_fall.last_major_event: IRGC claimed to have struck a US F-35 stealth fighter over central Iran on April 3 while Iran's Foreign Minister stated Tehran was reviewing a US 15-point peace proposal without intent to negotiate, signaling parallel military escalation and diplomatic positioning.
